title: Meat Industry (Livestock and Carcass Grading) Regulations, 2010
title_en: Meat Industry (Livestock and Carcass Grading) Regulations, 2010

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Meat Industry (livestock and carcass grading ) Regulations, 2010.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These regulations shall apply to livestock intended for marketing, slaughter, meat and meat products at a registered establishment .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these regulations unless the context otherwise requires— “ abattoir-identification code ” means a code used on roller-mark of carcasses as well as on stamps for the identification of the abattoir of origin of the carcass ; “ age classification ” means the classification of livestock or a carcass as determined by age of the animal; “ carcass ” means the dressed carcass derived from slaughtered animals after removing hide or skin or hair in case of the pig or feathers in case of poultry , intestines, the pluck, the shanks and head, the diaphragm and lactating udders, in the case of cattle, sheep and goats and the tail in the case of cattle; “ chicken ” means a bird of the species Gallus domesticus having flexible cartilage at the posterior end of the breast or keel bone, tender meat and soft skin of smooth texture; “ conformation ” means a manner of animal formation with general reference to the animal’s body proportions and its ratio of edible tissue to bone; “ conformation classification ” means the classification of a carcass in respect of muscling ; “ carcass cutting establishment ” means an establishment in which beef, ovine, caprine and veal carcasses are divided into primal and sub-primal cuts and pig carcasses are divided into principal cuts; “ damage classification ” means the classification of a carcass in respect of injury; “ director ” means the director responsible for the animal production under the ministry responsible for livestock; “ establishment number ” means the number assigned to an establishment registered under the Act; “ establishment ” means a registered livestock market, abattoir or a meat processing facility under these regulations; “ fat thickness ” means the thickness of the back fat and in case of a pig carcass it includes the skin; “ grade ” means the quality characteristics of a livestock, livestock carcasses or meat ; “ grading equipment ” means designated equipment used in grading ; “ grader ” means a person appointed for the purpose of grading livestock, carcasses or meat product; “ grader’s code ” means the number assigned to a grader that appears on every grade stamp or yield stamp used by the grader ; “ grading ” means the determination of class, grade , or other quality of livestock, livestock carcasses or meat products according to applicable standards for such livestock species; “ grading certificate ” means a certificate issued to the owner giving information on livestock carcass and meat grades in accordance with regulation 6 ; “ grading stand ” means a platform used by a grader when grading livestock carcasses and meat ; “ identification card ” means a card issued to an inspector or grader under regulation 11 the purpose of identification; “ inspector ” means a person appointed regulation 10 for the purpose of inspecting livestock production units, livestock markets, certificate of registration, license, record book, electronic information storage system or any other document in a premises; “ knife-rib ” means to cut the left side, or the left and right sides of a carcass by severing the vertebrae and cutting 15 cm or more beyond the Longissimus muscles in order to expose them for evaluation by a grader ; “ livestock farm ” means an area of land with or without structures used for the purpose of raising livestock for meat production; “ livestock production unit ” means a registered livestock farm ; “ livestock trader ” means any person or group of persons, who buy livestock from a registered producer or dealer for sale or slaughter for public consumption; “ meat ” means the whole or part of the dressed carcass of am slaughtered livestock or non-conventional animal; “ marbling ” means the amount, size and distribution of intramuscular fat deposits in the Longissimus muscles of a livestock carcass that have been exposed by knife-ribbing; “ muscling ” means the general outline of the muscle formation of a livestock carcass ; “ official mark ” means any form of mark used under these regulations in marking graded livestock or carcasses; “ poultry carcass ” means an eviscerated or partially eviscerated carcass of poultry ; “ poultry ” means a bird of the species Gallus domesticus and includes a chicken , turkey, duck, goose or guinea fowl; “ primal cut ” in respect of a beef carcass means the hip, sirloin, short loin, rib or chunk of the side of the carcass , and in respect of an ovine, caprine or a veal carcass means the leg, loin or fore saddle of the side of the carcass ; “ quality grade ” means the characteristics of the meat which predicts the palatability of the lean; “ Registrar ” means the Registrar appointed under section 15 of the Act; “ sub-primal cut ” means a cut of meat that is greater than 125 cm and is derived from a beef carcass or a primal cut thereof; “ weight ” means the live weight of a livestock and warm weight of a livestock carcass as determined using a weighing scale approved by authority responsible for weights and measures; “ yield ” means the estimated percentage of lean meat in the beef and lamb carcass derived from the primal cuts, the brisket, the Hank, the plate and the shank of the carcass ; “ yield grade ” means closely trimmed boneless meat from the major wholesale cuts (round, sirloin, short loin, rib, and square-cut chunk) of a carcass . Part II – Administration

provisions.title: Terms and conditions of grading
provisions.content: (1) A grader shall grade livestock or carcasses with designated grading equipment , only if the equipment is functioning properly and is accurate. (2) A grader shall grade livestock or carcass at an establishment in the presence of— (a) a person in authority at an establishment ; (b) the producer; (c) the agent of the producer; or (d) the person in possession of the livestock or carcasses. (3) All establishments shall be allocated identification by the Board for use in marking grades. (4) Subject to sub-regulation (3) , the identification code shall only be used in an establishment to which it has been allocated and not otherwise. (5) The Board may withdraw an establishment identification code if— (a) the marking is not done in accordance with these Regulations; (b) the registration certificate issued to the establishment has been withdrawn by. (6) Subject to sub-regulation (5) all marking equipment shall, within forty-eight hours be handed over to the Board for safekeeping until— (a) the establishment apply for the same or a new identification code has been granted, as the case may be; (b) the Board decides to give the roller-mark equipment to another establishment .

provisions.title: Grading certificate
provisions.content: A grading certificate described in the First Schedule shall be issued by a grader in respect of a carcass or a lot of carcasses, upon meeting the standards set out in these regulations. Part III – Grading of livestock

provisions.title: Grade classes of cattle, sheep, goats or pigs
provisions.content: (1) There shall be Tanzania Grades for cattle, sheep, goats, pigs and chicken based on live weight , age, species category, conformation , and back fat thickness in case of pigs as set out in part (a)(b) and (c) of the Second Schedule (b), and (c) for cattle or Pigs sheep and goat and pigs respectively. (2) A livestock shall not be graded unless at the time of delivery to the market, the producer or the agent of the producer has— (a) identified each animal to be sold with a distinct identification number; (b) completed and filed a manifest that identifies each animal by its producer. Part IV – Grading of carcasses

provisions.title: Grading of carcasses
provisions.content: (1) There shall be carcass grades based on age, species category, conformation , marbling , fatness, fat thickness in case of pigs as set out in parts (a), (b), (c), (d), (e) of the Third Schedule and in case of poultry carcasses shall base on physical appearance and colour of carcass as prescribed in the fourth Schedule. (2) A carcass shall not be graded unless— (a) at the time of delivery of the animal to the establishment for slaughter, the producer or the agent of the producer has— (i) provided a distinct identification number of the slaughtered animal; or (ii) completed and filed a manifest that identifies each animal by its producer. (b) a person in authority at the establishment has, after slaughter, retained on each carcass or transferred thereto an identification number referred to subparagraph (a)(i) .

provisions.title: Marking of carcasses
provisions.content: (1) A carcass shall not be marked unless it is graded as prescribed in the Third Schedules (a), (b), (c), (d). (2) A person other than a grader shall not apply an official mark to a livestock carcass or a primal cut . (3) Subject to sub-regulation (2) , an official mark shall not be removed from a livestock carcass or a primal cut unless— (a) the removal is under the supervision of a grader ; or (b) the carcass or primal cut is being trimmed for further processing. (4) Where fat to which an official mark has been applied is removed from a carcass or a primal cut in accordance with subsection (3) (a) or (b) , the fat shall be disposed off or destroyed to the satisfaction of a grader. (5) A grader shall not mark a livestock carcass using ink that is not approved for that purpose. Part V – Appointment, duties and powers of inspenction and grades

provisions.title: Appointment of graders and inspectors
provisions.content: (1) Inspector shall, upon recommendation of the Registrar , appoint from amongst Livestock Professionals and Livestock Field Officers in public service to be graders or inspectors. (2) The Inspector or Grader shall be trained on grading or inspection skills.

provisions.title: Identification cards
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar shall, immediately after appointment of Inspectors and Graders under regulation 9 issue the identification card for each Inspector and Grader. (2) The identification card issued under sub-regulation (1) shall be in the form prescribed in the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Tenure of office of inspector and grader
provisions.content: (1) An inspector or grader appointed under these Regulations shall, unless dies or resign or retires, hold an office for a term of five years and may be eligible for re-appointment. (2) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (1) , the Director may at any time before the expiry of five years and upon recommendation of the Registrar, terminate the service of the inspector or grader.

provisions.title: Duties of Inspector
provisions.content: (1) An Inspector appointed under these Regulations shall have the duty to perform such functions and to exercise such powers as may be directed by the Registrar . (2) Without prejudice to the generality of sub-regulation (1) duties of inspectors shall include— (a) to inspect livestock production units and livestock markets within their respective area of operation; (b) to examine or inspect any certificate of registration, license, record book, electronic information storage system or any other document in the premises for that purpose, he may do such other things including taking of extracts from documents in the possession of that person, as may be necessary to effect the examination or inspection. (3) An inspector shall immediately after effecting inspection under these Regulations prepare and submit a report on his findings to the Registrar . (4) The Report prepared under sub-regulation (1) shall be in the form prescribed in part (a) of the Fifth Schedule. (5) The Registrar shall consider the report submitted under sub regulation (2) and take action if any and submit to the Board and inform the Director.

provisions.title: Powers of Inspector
provisions.content: The Inspector shall, in the performance of his functions have powers— (a) to enter any livestock production unit or market facility or premises for the purpose of inspection; (b) to require the owner of the facility or premises for that purpose to observe and maintain good condition or established standards of the livestock production unit or market facility; (c) to issue prohibition notice to an owner or occupier of livestock production unit or market facility who operates in contravention of prescribed procedures; (d) seize and detain any equipment, record, or any other thing which appear to him to constitute or contain evidence or a contravention of any provision of this rule and fill in seize form as described in Sixth Schedule; (e) close the premises found to contravene the law and institute criminal proceedings; (f) to inquire into any matter regarding livestock production or livestock marketing within their areas.

provisions.title: Duties of grader
provisions.content: (1) A grader appointed under these Regulations shall have the duty to perform such functions and to exercise such powers as may be directed by the Board. (2) Without prejudice to the generality of sub-regulation (1) duties of graders shall include— (a) to grade all livestock brought for sale at a livestock markets assigned to him; and (b) to grade all carcasses slaughtered in an establishment assigned to him. (3) The grader shall immediately after grading livestock or carcasses, prepare and submit a report of the grades to the Registrar and keep a copy in the establishment . (4) The Report prepared under sub-regulation (3) shall be in the form prescribed in the Seventh Schedule. (5) The Registrar shall consider the report submitted under sub regulation (3) and take action, if any, and submit the report to the Board and the Director. (6) The Grader shall put a mark on a graded carcass or livestock indicating the applicable grade .

provisions.title: Powers of grader
provisions.content: The grader shall, in the performance of his duties have powers— (a) to enter any livestock marketing or establishment for the purpose of grading livestock or carcass ; and (b) to require the owner of livestock and carcass to allow his livestock and meat to be graded at an establishment .

provisions.title: Termination of services of the inspector or grader
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar may recommend to the Director termination of inspector and grader ’s services where fails without good cause— (a) to perform his duties; or (b) to observe any of the conditions attached on his identification card , and recommend for appointment of a new inspector or grader in his place. (2) Any inspector and grader who has been terminated or resigned or retires from his office shall within seven days from that date submit the identification card issued to him to the Registrar of the Board. (3) An inspector and grader who fails to submit the identification card within the period specified under sub-regulation (2) commits an offence. Part VI – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Restriction
provisions.content: (1) A person shall not trade livestock or carcass or meat in Tanzania Mainland unless; it is graded and marked as prescribed under these Regulations. (2) A person shall not import meat into Tanzania Mainland unless, is graded and marked. (3) An official mark or other method of expression that directly or by implication constitutes a misrepresentation shall not be marked on a carcass , container or outer container of meat . (4) The words "super", "prime", "top", "choice", "quality", "extra" or any other word or expression which directly or by implication creates or may create the impression that meat or a carcass is of a special or particular quality other than a characteristic referred to in these regulations, shall not be marked on the container of meat or stamped on a carcass . (5) A grader shall not grade a carcass at an establishment or a cutting establishment , as the case may be, unless, al the time of grading , the establishment or cutting establishment has facilities and equipment for weighing and grading carcasses. (6) A grader shall not grade a carcass unless— (a) it bears a meat inspection legend stamp or, in the case of an imported beef carcass , the official inspection mark of the government of the country of origin; (b) grading is done at the establishment in which the animal was slaughtered; (c) has been weighed by an approved weigh master using an approved scale; (d) is presented for grading at a grading stand where the lighting intensity is sufficient enough and in a cool place. (7) A grader when grading a carcass shall not assign a grade to the carcass unless it meets the standards set out in the Regulations for that grade . (8) A grader shall not grade a beef carcass unless the carcass has, at least 10 minutes before grading and to the satisfaction of the grader , been knife-ribbed by an employee of the establishment . (9) A grader shall not grade a veal carcass unless an employee of the establishment or cutting establishment , as the case may be, has, at least 10 minutes before grading and to the satisfaction of the grader , made a fresh cut on the lean of the brisket in order to enable the grader to determine the colour reading of the veal carcass . (10) An employee of an establishment shall not trim a carcass before it is weighed, except on the direction of a meat inspector . (11) A grader shall not grade an eviscerated poultry carcass unless— (a) the carcass is from poultry slaughtered in an establishment ; (b) the carcass has been inspected under the Animal Disease Act that provides for the inspection of processed poultry ; [ Cap. 156 ] (c) the flesh or skin of a chilled carcass is not dried out; (d) the carcass is not discoloured from insufficient draining of blood; (e) the carcass has no more than one heart, liver, gizzard and neck packed with it or inserted into it; (f) the breast bone is intact, in the case of a chicken carcass weighing more than 900g.

provisions.title: Offence and penalties
provisions.content: (1) Any person who contravenes or fails to comply with any of the provisions of these regulations shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not less than fifty thousand Tanzanian shillings or to imprisonment for a term not less than six months or to both fine and imprisonment. (2) Where an offence under the Act or these Regulations is a subsequent offence and to which no penalty is provided in respect of the continuance thereof, the person who commits that offence, shall in addition to any other penalty, be liable to a fine of not less than one million Tanzanian shillings or to imprisonment to a term of not less than twelve months or to both such fine and imprisonment. (3) A person who— (a) wilfully delays or obstructs any inspector / grader in the exercise of his powers under this rule; or (b) refuses or fails without reasonable cause to give any information which he is lawfully required to give under this sub-rule; and (c) gives any information, which is false in a material particular or which he reasonably knows to be untrue; commits an offence and shall upon conviction be liable to a fine not less than fifty thousand Tanzanian shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding 6 months or to both such fine and imprisonment. B 5-7 Bull 200-350 AB, B 5-7 Tanzania 2(Commercial) Cow 250-350 B, C 8-9 Bull 350-500 B, C 8-9 Bullock 350-500 B, C 8-9 Tanzania 3 (Standard) Cow 180-350 B, C 3.5-4 Bull 200 - 500 B, C 3.5-4 Bullock 350-500 B, C 3.5-4 Tanzania 4(Utility) Steer Less than 100 A, AB, 2-3 Heifer Less than 80 A, AB, 2-3 Cow Less than 180 A, AB, B, C 2-3 Young Bull Less than 200 A, AB, B 2-3 Bull Less than 300 C 2-3 Bullock Less than 350 C 2-3 Note: Age classification of Cattle Class “calf’ means calf of less than 15 months of age. Class A means cattle with no permanent incisor of 15 to 18 months of age. Class B means cattle with 1 to 2 permanent incisors of 18 to 24 months of age. Class AB means cattle with 1 to 6 permanent incisors of 24 to 30 months of age. Class C cattle with more than 6 permanent incisors of more than 30 months of age. Description of Body Condition Scores Score Condition Feature 1 L Marked emaciation (animal would be condemned at ante mortem examination) 2 L Transverse processes project prominently, neutral spines appear sharply 3 L Individual dorsal spines are pointed to the touch; hips, pins, tail-head and ribs are prominent. Transverse processes visible, usually individually 4 M- Ribs, hips and pines clearly visible. Muscle mass between hooks and pins slightly concave Slightly more flesh above the transverse processes than in L+ 5 M Rib usually visible, little fat cover, dorsal spines barely visible 6 M+ Animal smooth and well covered; dorsal spines cannot be seen, but are easily felt 7 F- Animal smooth and well covered, but fat deposits are not marked. Dorsal spines can be felt with firm pressure, but feel rounded rather than sharp 8 F+ Fat cover in critical areas can be easily seen and felt: transverse processes cannot be seen or felt. 9 F+ Heavy deposits of fat clearly visible on tail-head, brisket and cod; dorsal spines, ribs, hooks and pins fully covered and cannot be felt even firm pressure Second Schedule (b) (Made under regulation 6) Tanzania sheep and goat grades TSGG Relative conformation score Conformation characteristics 1 Highest Thick and pronounced outside leg (Biceps femurs and semitendinosus) Full back, strip (rib and loin, Longissimus dorsi) Thick to outside shoulder (Triceps brachii group); 2 Middle slightly thick and a slightly pronounced outside leg (Biceps femoris and Semitendinosus); Slightly full (flat or slightly shallow) back strip (rib and loin, Longissimus dorsi).
